laws.title: Public Law 35-252 | Magistrate Appointment Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: Section 3 amends Public Law 26-190 section 106(c)(i), but it does not identify a clean positive-law U.S. Code rewrite target.

current_implementation.detail: The branch note is at a plausible Title 28 anchor, but the source is a prior-public-law amendment rather than a direct U.S. Code rewrite.

laws.recommended_action: Keep it as a statutory note at the closest Title 28 anchor and do not treat it as a direct code rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 35-254 | An Act to repeal Public Law 28-205
laws.status_basis: The source is an operative repealer and expressly states that Public Law 28-205 is repealed.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML treats the repealer itself as historical only, but the law is the operative repeal of Public Law 28-205.

laws.recommended_action: Retain it as the operative repealer and mark Public Law 28-205 fully repealed.

laws.title: Public Law 35-256 | Workers First Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The source reduces the income tax rate, but it does not name a determinable U.S. Code target in the accessible text.

current_implementation.detail: The note is a plausible anchor, but the source does not identify a precise U.S. Code target.

laws.recommended_action: Keep it as a statutory note unless a precise Code target is later identified.

laws.title: Public Law 36-256 | Commerce Dispersal Act
laws.status_basis: The source dissolves the Department of Commerce and Labor, redistributes its functions, and transfers copyright administration.

current_implementation.detail: The note is in the right subject area, but it still needs to be reduced to a concise transfer history.

laws.recommended_action: Keep only a concise transfer note and remove the full-text dump.

laws.title: Public Law 36-257 | Maritime Operations & Policy Overhaul Act
laws.status_basis: The source combines direct amendments to prior laws with uncodified operational and coordination directives.

current_implementation.detail: The note is directionally right, but it should be trimmed to concise amendment notes and not expanded into permanent text.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the Coast Guard amendments as concise amendment notes and do not turn the uncodified directives into permanent Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 36-258 | National Root Beer Day Act
laws.status_basis: The source makes a direct insertion into 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) by adding National Root Beer Day after Memorial Day.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is parked at Title 5 section 101, but the source requires a real insertion in 5 U.S.C. 6103(a).

laws.recommended_action: Apply the 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) insertion and keep the short title and effective date as note material.

laws.title: Public Law 36-260 | Fixing the District of Columbia Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The source combines explicit repeals, transfers, and amendments affecting multiple District laws and offices.

current_implementation.detail: The current note flattens multiple repeals, transfers, and amendments into one bloated entry.

laws.recommended_action: Record the six repeals and the listed transfer and amendment notes explicitly, and remove the bloated note.

laws.title: Public Law 37-261 | Federal Criminal Code Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The accessible source adopts an attached Federal Criminal Code, but the attachment text is not determinable from the accessible extraction.

current_implementation.detail: The note is at the right general anchor, but the attached code text cannot be reproduced from the accessible source and the note still needs cleanup.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the law as a source-limited statutory note at the existing anchor and do not invent unattested attached-code text.

laws.title: Public Law 38-262 | Fund the Government Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The source includes temporary appropriations, a permanent repeal of prior schedule rules, and a general schedule pay-scale provision without a precise Code target.

current_implementation.detail: The branch note is directionally correct for the appropriations and repeal history, but it still carries the full dump and should remain concise note-level history.

laws.recommended_action: Keep only a concise statutory note for the appropriations and repeal history; if the pay-scale table is meant to be permanent, map it to the proper Title 5 provision.

laws.title: Public Law 38-263 | National Security Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act creates an ongoing national-security framework and also repeals prior CRACK Act titles I-IV, but it does not self-expire.

current_implementation.detail: The note is at the title-50 anchor, but it still embeds the full dump and should stay note-only rather than integrated text.

laws.recommended_action: Replace the dump with a concise title-50 note and preserve the repeal history cleanly.
